title: Red-flag emergency short-circuit (Gate 2, 0 LLM calls)
description: The headline safety guarantee. Implement a deterministic, pure/synchronous RedFlagDetector (categories cardiac|stroke|anaphylaxis|self-harm|overdose|none with a patternsetVersion) and the real GuardrailSet.evaluate (allow|short-circuit|refuse with rulesetVersion) wrapping it, wired into MedicalSopEngine.run immediately AFTER the consent gate and BEFORE any numerics or LLM call. A red-flag match returns a short-circuit verdict with a canned 911/988 escalation and makes ZERO downstream calls (no numerics, no retrieval, no LLM). The short-circuit is audited with ruleId + versions. This replaces the allow-only GuardrailSet stub from Sprints 1-2.

successCriteria.description: The project builds with zero TypeScript errors (npm run build).
TypeScript strict-mode typecheck passes with zero errors (npm run typecheck).
All tests pass including new RedFlagDetector + GuardrailSet tests; the full pre-existing suite stays green.

For at least one representative prompt in EACH red-flag category (cardiac, stroke, anaphylaxis, self-harm, overdose), GuardrailSet.evaluate returns a short-circuit verdict with a canned escalation, and MedicalSopEngine.run makes ZERO LLM calls and ZERO numerics calls — verified with spy/fake dependencies asserted never invoked.
RedFlagDetector.detect is pure and synchronous (no async, no I/O, no network, no model): identical input yields identical RedFlagMatch across repeated calls, asserted by a test.
A non-emergency prompt returns category 'none' and the SOP proceeds past the guardrail to normal (allow) handling.
A short-circuit appends an audit entry with event 'short-circuit' carrying ruleId, rulesetVersion, and patternsetVersion (IDs/enums only — no prompt text).
GuardrailSet.evaluate throws on an empty prompt; the detector and guardrail require no network and no LLMClient (a test confirms no provider is constructed).

nonGoals: Do not implement HealthDataStore or numerics (Sprint 4) — the numerics dependency in run may remain a fake/stub this sprint.
Do not implement EgressGuard, medications, or the full retrieval SOP (Sprint 6).
Do not use an LLM for red-flag classification — detection is deterministic and local ONLY.
stopConditions: Every red-flag category short-circuits with a canned escalation and 0 LLM/numerics calls; 'none' proceeds; short-circuit is audited; evaluate throws on empty prompt
definitionOfDone: src/medical/ gains RedFlagDetector (pure detect(prompt): RedFlagMatch over a versioned pattern set, categories cardiac|stroke|anaphylaxis|self-harm|overdose|none) and a real GuardrailSet implementation whose evaluate(prompt, ctx) runs the detector first and returns short-circuit{rule,cannedResponse} on a match, refuse{rule,reason} for code-enforced refusals, else allow. MedicalSopEngine.run runs consent (S2) then GuardrailSet.evaluate; a short-circuit returns the canned escalation MedicalAnswer (shortCircuit=true) with the disclaimer footer + a 'short-circuit' audit entry and reaches NO numerics/LLM. Build, typecheck, and all tests pass.
assumptions: RedFlagMatch = { category: 'cardiac'|'stroke'|'anaphylaxis'|'self-harm'|'overdose'|'none'; ruleId?: string }. patternsetVersion is a string constant exported by the detector.

The pattern set is conservative keyword/phrase matching (case-insensitive, word-boundary aware) — deliberately favoring escalation reliability over paraphrase coverage (architecture ADR-2 accepts possible misses on novel phrasing).
self-harm/overdose escalate to 988 (US Suicide & Crisis Lifeline); cardiac/stroke/anaphylaxis escalate to 911. Canned responses are fixed strings, not model-generated.
The numerics dependency may be a fake this sprint; the LLMClient is injected so a spy can assert zero calls.
generatorNotes:
Add src/medical/red-flag.ts (RedFlagDetector + patternsetVersion) and src/medical/guardrails.ts (the real GuardrailSet replacing the S1 allow-only stub), collocated *.test.ts.

RedFlagDetector.detect MUST be a pure sync function: normalize the prompt (lowercase, trim), test against a per-category list of conservative patterns (word-boundary regexes or phrase includes), return the first matching category + a ruleId, else { category:'none' }. NO async, NO fs, NO network, NO LLM import. Export a patternsetVersion string (e.g. 'redflag-2026.06.16'). Keep categories: cardiac (e.g. 'chest pain' + radiating/short of breath), stroke (FAST: face droop, arm weakness, slurred speech, 'sudden numbness'), anaphylaxis ('throat closing', 'trouble breathing' + 'allergic'/sting/peanut), self-harm ('kill myself','suicidal','end my life'), overdose ('overdose','took too many','too much'). Favor reliability; document that novel phrasing may miss (ADR-2 risk).

GuardrailSet.evaluate(prompt, ctx): throw on empty/whitespace prompt; run detector; if category !== 'none' return { kind:'short-circuit', rule: ruleId, cannedResponse: escalationFor(category) }; (refuse branch for non-emergency code-enforced refusals can be a documented placeholder this sprint or minimal); else { kind:'allow' }. Expose rulesetVersion (e.g. 'guardrail-2026.06.16').

escalationFor(category): fixed strings. 911 for cardiac/stroke/anaphylaxis ('This may be a medical emergency. Call 911 (or your local emergency number) now.'), 988 for self-harm/overdose ('If you are in crisis, call or text 988 (US Suicide & Crisis Lifeline) now, or 911 if you are in immediate danger.'). NEVER model-generated.

MedicalSopEngine.run wiring: after consent gate, call this.guardrails.evaluate(prompt, ctx). On short-circuit: append audit { event:'short-circuit', ruleId, rulesetVersion, patternsetVersion } and return MedicalAnswer { body: cannedResponse, abstained:false, shortCircuit:true, citations:[], disclaimerFooter: footer() } WITHOUT calling numerics or the LLM. On allow: proceed to the placeholder normal path (numerics fake + footer). Inject the LLMClient and numerics layer so tests can spy.

Tests: parametrized over the 5 categories asserting short-circuit + spy LLM/numerics zero calls + audit entry; determinism (call detect twice, deep-equal); 'none' proceeds; empty prompt throws; assert guardrails.ts has no provider/network import.
evaluatorNotes:
sc-3-4: for each category, feed a representative emergency prompt to MedicalSopEngine.run with a spy LLMClient and a spy numerics layer; assert result.shortCircuit===true, body contains the right hotline (911 vs 988), and BOTH spies have zero calls.
sc-3-5: call detect(samePrompt) twice; deepEqual the results; assert the function is synchronous (not returning a Promise) and red-flag.ts imports nothing from src/providers or any network module.
sc-3-6: a benign prompt ('what was my average resting heart rate last week') => detect 'none' and run proceeds to the allow path.
sc-3-7: after a short-circuit, read the audit file; assert an entry with event 'short-circuit', a ruleId, rulesetVersion, patternsetVersion, and NO prompt text.
sc-3-8: evaluate('') and evaluate('   ') throw.
Full suite + build + typecheck green; confirm S1/S2 still pass (consent gate still fires before the guardrail).
